sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Suspicious Loading of Dbgcore/Dbghelp DLLs from Uncommon Location
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects loading of dbgcore.dll or dbghelp.dll from uncommon locations such as user directories.
These DLLs contain the MiniDumpWriteDump function, which can be abused for credential dumping purposes or in some cases for evading EDR/AV detection by suspending processes.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: ADS Zone.Identifier Deleted By Uncommon Application
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the deletion of the "Zone.Identifier" ADS by an uncommon process. Attackers can leverage this in order to bypass security restrictions that make use of the ADS such as Microsoft Office apps.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The sample is a malicious loader that downloads a PE binary from a hardcoded URL (https://github.com/g3tsyst3m/undertheradar/raw/refs/heads/main/putty.exe) and executes it directly in memory using reflective loading techniques. Key behaviors include using WinInet APIs to download the payload, manually mapping PE sections into memory allocated via VirtualAlloc, patching relocations, and resolving the Import Address Table (IAT) before jumping to the entry point. This methodology is designed to bypass file-based security scanners by avoiding writing the payload to disk.

Package: CMVLite2 (xpi)

Description: 

The provided source code consists entirely of configuration files (JSON, CFG, TXT, XML, Properties), image assets (PNG), binary data files (DAT), and compressed archives (ZIP) containing further configuration and assets. There are also PDF documents, likely containing informational content. The file structure and content are consistent with a Minecraft modpack. While some metadata indicates the use of Forge Mod Loader (FML) core plugins that perform bytecode transformations, this is a standard and legitimate practice in the Minecraft modding ecosystem for deep game integration and is not inherently malicious. No executable scripts or direct malicious code patterns were found. The package appears to be a collection of game assets and configurations, posing no direct security threat based on the provided content.

android.permission.CHANGE_NETWORK_STATE.full_description: Allows the app to change the state of network connectivity.

android.permission.READ_LOGS.full_description:
Allows the app to read from the
        system's various log files.  This allows it to discover general
        information about what you are doing with the phone, potentially
        including personal or private information.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.short_description: access approximate location (network-based) only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.full_description: This app can get your location based on network sources such as cell towers and Wi-Fi networks, but only when the app is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH.full_description:
Allows the app to view the
      configuration of the Bluetooth on the phone, and to make and accept
      connections with paired devices.

android.permission.ACCESS_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view information
      about Wi-Fi networking, such as whether Wi-Fi is enabled and name of
      connected Wi-Fi devices.

android.permission.INTERNET.full_description:
Allows the app to create
     network sockets and use custom network protocols. The browser and other
     applications provide means to send data to the internet, so this
     permission is not required to send data to the internet.

android.permission.CHANGE_CONFIGURATION.full_description:
Allows the app to
        change the current configuration, such as the locale or overall font
        size.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH_ADMIN.full_description:
Allows the app to configure
      the local Bluetooth phone, and to discover and pair with remote devices.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.short_description: access precise location only in the foreground
android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.full_description: This app can get your exact location only when it is in the foreground. These location services must be turned on and available on your phone for the app to be able to use them. This may increase battery consumption.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.full_description: Allows the app to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to view
      information about network connections such as which networks exist and are
      connected.

android.permission.CHANGE_WIFI_MULTICAST.full_description:
Allows the app to receive
      packets sent to all devices on a Wi-Fi network using multicast addresses,
      not just your phone.  It uses more power than the non-multicast mode.

android.permission.READ_CALL_LOG.full_description: This app can read your call history.

android.permission.ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOC.full_description: If this is granted additionally to the approximate or precise location access the app can access the location while running in the background.

android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.short_description: modify or delete the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAG.full_description: Allows the app to write the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.short_description: read the contents of your shared storage
android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.full_description: Allows the app to read the contents of your shared storage.

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.full_description:
Allows the app to
        have itself started as soon as the system has finished booting.
        This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the
        app to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.CALL_PHONE.full_description:
Allows the app to call phone numbers
      without your intervention. This may result in unexpected charges or calls.
      Note that this doesn't allow the app to call emergency numbers.
      Malicious apps may cost you money by making calls without your
      confirmation.

android.permission.WRITE_SETTINGS.full_description:
Allows the app to modify the
        system's settings data. Malicious apps may corrupt your system's
        configuration.

android.permission.FLASHLIGHT.full_description: Allows the app to control the flashlight.

android.permission.READ_PHONE_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to access the phone
      features of the device.  This permission allows the app to determine the
      phone number and device IDs, whether a call is active, and the remote number
      connected by a call.

android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS.full_description: Allows the app to modify global audio settings such as volume and which speaker is used for output.

android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE.full_description: Allows the app to make use of foreground services.

android.permission.READ_SMS.short_description: read your text messages (SMS or MMS)
android.permission.READ_SMS.full_description: This app can read all SMS (text) messages stored on your phone.

android.permission.VIBRATE.full_description: Allows the app to control the vibrator.

android.permission.SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW.short_description: This app can appear on top of other apps
android.permission.SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW.full_description: This app can appear on top of other apps or other parts of the screen. This may interfere with normal app usage and change the way that other apps appear.

android.permission.KILL_BACKGROUND_PROCE.full_description:
Allows the app to end
      background processes of other apps.  This may cause other apps to stop
      running.

android.permission.CAMERA.full_description: This app can take pictures and record videos using the camera at any time.

android.permission.REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKA.full_description: Allows an application to request installation of packages.

android.permission.CHANGE_WIFI_STATE.full_description:
Allows the app to connect to and
      disconnect from Wi-Fi access points and to make changes to device
      configuration for Wi-Fi networks.

android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO.full_description: This app can record audio using the microphone at any time.

android.permission.MOUNT_UNMOUNT_FILESYS.full_description:
Allows the app to mount and
        unmount filesystems for removable storage.

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potentially Suspicious CMD Shell Output Redirect
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects inline Windows shell commands redirecting output via the ">" symbol to a suspicious location.
This technique is sometimes used by malicious actors in order to redirect the output of reconnaissance commands such as "hostname" and "dir" to files for future exfiltration.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.

Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset.
